description: Letter from ʿAyyāsh b. Ṣadaqa, in Būṣīr, to Nahray b. Nissim, in Fustat. Dating (Gil): ca. 1045 CE. ʿAyyāsh is about to finish the phase of buying flax in Būṣīr. The letter contains details about market difficulties (extremely high prices for flax, problems with suppliers, quality issues); business operations (partnership arrangements with Burhān and Yūsuf; weight measurements and accounting); types of textiles (type and quality distinctions for flax and finished cloth). The letter complains of significant market stress in the flax trade, with high prices preventing normal business operations and quality concerns affecting trade relationships. Verso: Account by ʿAyyāsh for business with his partners. Also mentions a Christian named Yuhannis. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #475) VMR/MR
description_he: מכתב מעיאש בן צדקה, בבוסיר, לנהראי בן ניסים, פוסטאט, כ-1045, על קשיי שוק הפשתן ומחירים גבוהים מאד ועל שותפות עם ברהון ויוסף
